What kind of liability do Partners have under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004?
column4: "Under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004, each Partner is liable jointly and severally with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all the other Partners."
column6: "Each Partner is liable jointly and severally with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner."

column2: Under the Foundations Law DIFC Law No. 3 of 2018, what is the maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days?
column4: "The maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days is $1,500. This is based on Article 10(7) of the Foundations Law, which specifies a fine for a person failing to provide a copy of assignment of rights."

column2: Under the Operating Law - DIFC Law No. 7 of 2018, who is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar?
column4: "Under the Operating Law, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar. Specifically, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA shall appoint an individual to serve as the Registrar and may dismiss such a person from the office of Registrar. However, prior to appointing or dismissing the Registrar, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA must consult the President."
column6: "The Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar."

Which specific DIFC Laws were amended by DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022?
column4: "Based on the provided context, DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022 amended the following specific DIFC Laws: the Common Reporting Standard Law (DIFC Law No. 2 of 2018), the Employment Law (DIFC Law No. 2 of 2019), the General Partnership Law (DIFC Law No. 11 of 2004), the Law on the Application of Civil and Commercial Laws in the DIFC (DIFC Law No. 3 of 2004), the Personal Property Law (DIFC Law No. 9 of 2005), and the Limited Liability Partnership Law (DIFC Law No. 5 of 2004)."
column6: "DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022 amended the Common Reporting Standard Law (DIFC Law No. 2 of 2018) and the General Partnership Law (DIFC Law No. 11 of 2004)."

What is the effective date for due diligence requirements for Pre-existing Accounts and New Accounts under the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018?
column4: "The effective date for due diligence requirements for Pre-existing Accounts under the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018 is 31 December 2016, and for New Accounts it is 1 January 2017. This is based on Article 6 of the Law, which states these dates apply retrospectively."
column6: "The effective date for due diligence requirements for Pre-existing Accounts is 31 December, 2016, and for New Accounts, it is 1 January, 2017."

column2: According to Article 2 of the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005, who made this Law?
column4: "According to Article 2 of the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005, this Law was made by the Ruler of Dubai."
column6: This Law is made by the Ruler of Dubai.
